old_claim.note: Printed Standardized Textbooks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected node is anchored to Pacioli's 1494 printed accounting treatment in Summa; the broader printed-standardized-textbooks node is not retained as a direct prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: MAA identifies Pacioli's Summa as a 1494 Venice publication and describes its Particularis de computis et scripturis section as an early printed treatment of double-entry bookkeeping.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The merchant accounting manual node is directly supported by Pacioli's printed accounting section, not by a later generalized standardized-textbook node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the node as a printed bookkeeping/accounting treatment. Treating the broader printed textbook category as a separate prerequisite would make the node depend on a later abstraction rather than the direct print-and-accounting lineage.

ontology_before: Merchant Accounting Manuals depended directly on Printed Standardized Textbooks, conflating the accounting manual instance with a broader textbook infrastructure node.
ontology_after: Merchant Accounting Manuals depends directly on double-entry bookkeeping diffusion and is anchored to a source-backed printed accounting manual.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-22-merchant-accounting-manuals-scope keeps printed_standardized_textbooks absent as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to later standardized accounting textbooks as an educational infrastructure category.
A source showed Pacioli-style printed accounting manuals directly required a prior standardized-textbook infrastructure.
short_reason: The node is itself an early printed accounting manual, so a broader textbook category is not a direct prerequisite.
